2606.07226.title: DEFINED: A Data-Efficient Computational Framework for Fine-Grained Creativity Assessment in Debate Scenarios
2606.07226.paper_summary: Human creativity has emerged as a critical competency in the era of large language models. Assessing creativity in complex, open-ended environments is a grand challenge in data mining, currently hindered by a reliance on standardized simple tasks and the scarcity of fine-grained expert data. As an ecologically valid assessment context, debate reflects multiple dimensions of creativity, encompassing both divergent thinking and convergent thinking. Moreover, debate is a data-rich domain, with a large volume of publicly accessible materials. Current mainstream automated scoring methods are poorly suited to complex settings such as debate, and therefore still rely on costly human evaluation. To this end, this paper proposes DEFINED, a data-efficient computational framework for fine-grained creativity assessment in debate scenarios. DEFINED operationalizes debate creativity through a hierarchical eight-dimensional metric system, implemented via a pre-trained autoregressive language model with a hierarchical scoring head that supports both fine-grained and coarse-grained evaluation. Statements and their associated expert scores were obtained from authentic debate competitions, and a constrained data augmentation strategy was employed to address the elite bias inherent in the original data. DEFINED adopts a mixed-granularity training strategy enabling robust learning from limited fine-grained supervision annotated by trained graduate experts. To rigorously validate ecological validity beyond synthetic benchmarks, we incorporate an empirical study with debate-naive participants, utilizing these authentic data to serve as a qualitative case study for mid-to-low proficiency populations. Across our evaluation protocol, our scoring model achieves accurate and stable scoring, outperforming prompt-based large language model evaluators and existing debate scoring methods.
2606.07226.paper_summary_zh: 人類創造力已成為大型語言模型時代的一項關鍵能力。<lb/>在複雜的開放式環境中評估創造力是一項重大的挑戰，這在數據挖掘中受到依賴於標準化簡單任務和缺乏細粒度專家數據的限制。<lb/>作為一個生態有效的評估環境，辯論反映了創造力的多個維度，涵蓋了發散思維和聚合思維。<lb/>此外，辯論是一個數據豐富的領域，擁有大量可公開獲取的材料。<lb/>目前主流的自動評分方法不太適合像辯論這樣的複雜環境，因此仍然依賴昂貴的人為評估。<lb/>為此，本文提出了DEFINED，一個數據高效的計算框架，用於在辯論場景中進行細粒度的創造力評估。<lb/>DEFINED通過一個分層的八維度指標系統來操作辯論創造力，該系統通過一個預訓練的自回歸語言模型實現，並配備一個支持細粒度和粗粒度評估的分層評分頭。<lb/>聲明及其相關的專家分數來自真實的辯論比賽，並採用了受限的數據增強策略來解決原始數據中固有的精英偏見。<lb/>DEFINED採用混合粒度的訓練策略，使得能夠從受訓的研究生專家標註的有限細粒度監督中進行穩健學習。<lb/>為了嚴格驗證生態有效性，超越合成基準，我們納入了一項實證研究，參與者為對辯論不熟悉的人，利用這些真實數據作為中低水平人群的質性案例研究。<lb/>在我們的評估協議中，我們的評分模型實現了準確且穩定的評分，超越了基於提示的大型語言模型評估者和現有的辯論評分方法。
2606.07226.author: Tongzhou Yu et.al.
2606.07226.authors: Tongzhou Yu, Mingjia Li, Hong Qian, Wenkai Wang, Zongbao Zhang, Yaoyu Jiang, Xiangfeng Wang, Aimin Zhou, Jiajun Guo
